name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow is a leading enterprise cloud platform for digital workflows, providing IT service management, IT operations management, customer service, HR delivery, security operations, and AI-powered workflow automation to enterprises globally. The company completed a 5-for-1 stock split in December 2025. Founded in 2004 and headquartered in Santa Clara, California, ServiceNow serves government, financial services, healthcare, manufacturing, and technology sectors.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has transformed from an IT ticketing tool into the dominant enterprise workflow automation platform, successfully expanding into HR delivery, customer service, security operations, and now AI-powered agentic workflows over the past 5 years [1]. Subscription revenue grew from $7.2B (2022) to $13.3B (2025), a 22% CAGR, reflecting continuous product expansion. The 5-for-1 stock split in December 2025 reflected sustained execution. AI Deployment (2/2): ServiceNow has embedded AI across its entire platform with Now Assist (generative AI), AI Agents, and the agentic AI roadmap that CEO McDermott calls 'the most ambitious in enterprise software' [2]. 16 transactions over $5M in net new ACV in Q1 2026 (up ~80% YoY) demonstrate AI-driven deal expansion with measurable revenue impact [1]. Market Share (1/2): ServiceNow is the dominant ITSM platform globally with an estimated 40%+ market share and expanding into adjacent workflow markets. However, it faces competition from Microsoft, Salesforce, and emerging AI-native startups. Product Pipeline (1/2): The agentic AI roadmap and AI Agent control tower are clearly articulated with general timelines, though specific next-gen product launch dates with revenue targets are broad rather than pinpointed. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D spending runs at approximately 20% of revenue, well above the software industry average [4].

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose — 'making the world work better for everyone' — is specific, service-oriented, and connects the company's workflow automation technology to a larger vision of improved human productivity. Under McDermott's 6-year tenure, this purpose has been consistently referenced in strategic decisions and product development. The mission is clearly more than marketing, but it has not yet been tested through leadership transitions or multi-decade consistency [2]. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow's community programs include workforce development initiatives and digital skills training, but no distinctive foundation or charitable investment programs with specific dollar amounts were identified. Employee Retention (2/2): 80% of employees receive equity grants, with 95% of shares going to non-executives, creating broad ownership alignment [3]. McDermott's 'people before profits' philosophy and the employee-centric culture have been consistently validated by above-average retention for the software industry, with the company growing headcount to 29,187 while maintaining cultural coherence. Insider Buying (1/2): The $5 billion share repurchase authorization (January 2026) and $2 billion accelerated repurchase program signal institutional confidence, though this is corporate buyback rather than personal insider buying [3].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on growth stock metrics. Q1 2026 delivered subscription revenue of $3.67B (up 22% YoY), total revenue of $3.77B, and $27.7B in remaining performance obligations (up 25% YoY) [1]. D/E of 20.7% is conservative for a high-growth software company (score 1.0). ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target (score 1.0, 2x weight). Revenue growth of 22.1% YoY far exceeds the 10% target (score 1.0). 3-year CAGR of 22.4% is exceptional (score 1.0, 2x weight). Forward P/E of 20.3 is at the 20 target (score 0.99). Profit margin of 12.6% is below the 20% target (score 0.63) — the primary weakness, reflecting stock-based compensation burden. Forward PEG of 0.99 is at the 1.0 target, indicating rare growth-at-a-reasonable-price (score 1.0, 2x weight). Weighted sum 9.62/10 = 10 [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): ServiceNow's enterprise workflow platform has meaningful defensive moats — deep integration into customer IT environments, high switching costs, institutional workflow data, and regulatory/compliance dependencies that create stickiness [2]. However, AI-native competitors could theoretically build workflow automation from scratch using large language models, bypassing ServiceNow's platform approach. Microsoft's Copilot ecosystem poses the most credible threat, as enterprise customers already using M365 could consolidate workflow automation within Microsoft's stack. CEO McDermott acknowledges this by framing ServiceNow as the 'AI agent control tower,' positioning the platform as the orchestration layer rather than a point solution vulnerable to AI substitution. The risk is real but manageable — ServiceNow's data moats and enterprise entrenchment provide meaningful protection. AI Opportunity (1/1): ServiceNow generates substantial AI-driven revenue with 16 transactions over $5M net new ACV in Q1 2026 (up ~80% YoY) driven by Now Assist and agentic AI capabilities [1]. AI is accelerating deal sizes and expanding the total addressable market for the platform.

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under Bill McDermott exemplifies infinite game thinking. McDermott's 'people before profits' philosophy, consistent 22%+ growth sustained over multiple years without acquisitive shortcuts, and the organic platform expansion from ITSM to enterprise-wide workflow automation reflect a company playing to keep playing. The agentic AI strategy — positioning ServiceNow as the orchestration layer for autonomous enterprise workflows — represents a forward-looking bet on enduring relevance rather than quarterly optimization. McDermott's 6-year tenure has produced exceptional results while maintaining cultural coherence across 29,000+ employees.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): 'Making the world work better for everyone' is service-oriented, inclusive, and connects ServiceNow's technology to a larger purpose — reducing friction in how organizations operate so people can focus on higher-value work [2]. McDermott consistently references this purpose in strategic decisions. However, the cause has only been tested through one leadership era (McDermott since 2019), and a true 3-point Just Cause requires multi-decade validation through leadership transitions and crises.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): ServiceNow demonstrates exceptional trust through its equity compensation model — 80% of employees receive equity grants, with 95% of shares going to non-executives, creating the broadest ownership alignment in enterprise software [3]. McDermott's 'people before profits' mantra is backed by above-average retention, growing headcount to 29,187 while maintaining cultural coherence, and a 'create always' innovation culture that empowers teams to experiment. The 6-year stability under McDermott's leadership and absence of major layoffs during the 2023-2024 tech downturn (when peers cut aggressively) signal genuine organizational trust.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott explicitly frames competition as motivation rather than warfare, stating that AI is 'devouring' low-value vendors and positioning ServiceNow to absorb their capabilities [2]. Rather than attacking Microsoft or Salesforce directly, ServiceNow has differentiated by becoming the workflow orchestration layer that integrates with rivals' ecosystems. This approach — building on rivals' strengths rather than attacking their weaknesses — reflects the infinite mindset.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): ServiceNow has evolved significantly from a narrow ITSM tool to an enterprise-wide workflow automation platform spanning HR, customer service, security, and now AI agent orchestration [1]. Each expansion entered adjacent markets and required new capabilities. However, the evolution has been additive and incremental rather than abandoning a core business — ServiceNow still derives the bulk of revenue from ITSM-adjacent workflows. No transformative pivot equivalent to Apple abandoning the Mac for iPhone.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): McDermott's decision to invest aggressively in AI during the 2023-2024 macro uncertainty — while peers were cutting costs and headcount — represents genuine courage. The company maintained R&D spending at ~20% of revenue and avoided layoffs when the industry was shedding workers [2][3]. The $5 billion buyback authorization and $2 billion accelerated repurchase demonstrate confidence in long-term value creation. McDermott's consistent messaging of 'staying hungry and humble' while growing from $7B to $14B in revenue under his tenure validates courageous long-term investment over quarterly optimization.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow delivered Q1 2026 subscription revenue of $3.67B (up 22% YoY, accelerating from 19.5% in Q4), beat the high end of guidance on all metrics, and raised full-year subscription revenue guidance to $15.74-15.78B (20.5-21% growth) [1]. Remaining performance obligations of $27.7B (up 25%) provide exceptional revenue visibility. The company achieved 16 deals over $5M net new ACV (up ~80% YoY), driven by AI-powered workflow automation demand. With a PEG ratio of 0.99, ServiceNow offers rare growth-at-a-reasonable-price for a 22% grower with $5.1B in free cash flow and 76.6% gross margins. The agentic AI platform positioning as the enterprise 'AI agent control tower' expands the TAM from IT workflow to all enterprise workflows [2].
thesis_continued: The primary risk is valuation sustainability — at 61x trailing P/E, any deceleration below 20% growth would trigger significant multiple compression. GAAP profit margin of 12.6% is surprisingly low for a $14B revenue software company, reflecting heavy stock-based compensation that dilutes real shareholder returns despite the $5B buyback program [1]. EPS growth of just 2.3% YoY despite 22% revenue growth highlights the margin gap between subscription growth and bottom-line delivery. Microsoft's Copilot ecosystem represents the most credible competitive threat, as enterprise customers could consolidate workflow automation within the M365 stack rather than maintaining a separate ServiceNow platform. The company's geopolitical exposure — noted in the Q1 earnings call as a challenge — could impact enterprise IT spending in a macro downturn [1][3].
key_catalysts: Agentic AI adoption accelerating enterprise deal sizes, with $5M+ deals growing ~80% YoY and the platform positioning as the AI agent orchestration layer for autonomous enterprise workflows
Full-year subscription revenue guidance raised to $15.74-15.78B, with RPO of $27.7B providing 2+ years of revenue visibility and supporting sustained 20%+ growth
Free cash flow of $5.1B and $5B buyback program creating capital return optionality while maintaining aggressive R&D investment in AI capabilities
key_risks: Trailing P/E of 61x and GAAP profit margin of only 12.6% create valuation vulnerability — any growth deceleration below 20% would compress the multiple significantly
Microsoft Copilot ecosystem could consolidate enterprise workflow automation within the M365 stack, reducing demand for a separate ServiceNow platform at the margin
Heavy stock-based compensation depresses GAAP earnings growth (2.3% YoY despite 22% revenue growth), raising questions about real shareholder value creation versus employee equity dilution
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Financial Results and Raised Full-Year Guidance — ServiceNow Newsroom and SEC 8-K, April 2026
Bill McDermott Leadership, AI Strategy, and 'AI Agent Control Tower' Positioning — BBN Times, LEADERS Magazine, Cloud Wars, 2025-2026
ServiceNow Stock Split, $5B Buyback Authorization, Equity Compensation Structure — SEC Filings, 2025-2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed May 2026
